question: Rights amplification (object-capability): the client acquires a capability-minting facet (a sealer/unsealer pair or a factory) so that, relative to the SAME ambient access budget, it can now generate and compose capabilities it could not before. Is this a genuine capability enlargement distinct from a permission/access change, or does it collapse into an access grant?

construction.retention_statement: One declared construction: the ambient access budget (ACL entries, reachable resource set) is held fixed and the client's held authority acquires a GENERATIVE facet whose exercise enlarges the normalized realizable task set. A reading that treats acquiring the facet as merely one more permission grant is retained separately as the token-acquisition rival, not collapsed into this one.

branches.label: client acquires a capability-minting facet
branches.normalization_frame: Common task vocabulary is the set of effects realizable by the client holding the ambient access budget (ACL entries, reachable resources) FIXED. Under that held-fixed access, acquiring the generative minting facet strictly enlarges the realizable set, because the amplification is not mediated by any single access knob that could be normalized away.

assumptions: The acquired facet is generative (it mints or amplifies capabilities), so its value is not exhausted by any single reachable-resource grant.
The ambient access budget is held fixed in the normalized frame; the enlargement is scored against that fixed access rather than as a new ACL entry.
tests: Classify the acquisition of a generative authority whose normalized realizable set grows with ambient access held fixed as a capability enlargement, and check that it stays distinct from the access-change type.
falsifiers: A construction showing the facet's every effect is reproducible by a single ACL widening (so the normalized set is EQUAL once that one access is held fixed) would collapse this to an access change.
A pure pre/post relabeling changes the computed diagnosis.
nonclaims: This is a receiver-owned diagnosis of a stated object-capability rights-amplification scenario, not a new security theorem.

It does not claim the P2C static task-set discriminator reproduces the object-capability designation/authorization structural distinction; it claims only that, under the declared frame, a generative authority enlarges the normalized task set and is typed CAPABILITY_ENLARGEMENT.
reopening_conditions: Reopen if the amplification is shown reducible to a single access grant, or if acquiring the facet cannot be separated from an ambient access widening in any admissible frame.
